channel.title: Posts on zephyrtm
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on zephyrtm

item.title: my linux journey
item.description:
humble beginnings

back in 2024, i first discovered the magical land that is the Linux family of operating systems. it was when i bought a crappy second-hand laptop on my local equivalent of ebay with the prospect of fixing it. upon receiving it and turning it on, besides learning i probably should have cleaned out the fans first, i also noticed this was the previous owner’s school-managed laptop, and while i could have wiped the restricted copy of windows that was on it, i decided it would be more fun to see what i could do to jailbreak it. after some research, i stumbled upon a tool by the name of chntpw (Offline Windows Password & Registry Editor), which seemed to be able to fulfil the task of blanking the administrator password, and noticed that it was a Linux-only command line application. it was then that i first installed ubuntu, or rather used it from within the live environment (remember, i didn’t know much more about Linux than its name) and after a few attempts, succeeded in blanking the administrator password. as i was erasing all of that work with a fresh copy of windows 10, i remembered that other operating system i had used on the way, ubuntu. at that moment, i forced shutdown on the laptop, breaking the windows 10 installation process beyond repair, and decided that this 11.6″ quad-core 8gb RAM-having piece of oem-ware would be my experimentation machine from that moment onward.
